title: fix pull image that includes a sha
body:
when pulling an image that includes a sha such as:

centos/nginx-112-centos7@sha256:42330f7f29ba1ad67819f4ff3ae2472f62de13a827a74736a5098728462212e7

the final image name in libpod should not contain portions of the sha itself nor the sha
identifier.  and like docker, we provide a 'none' tag as well.

this should fix #877

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: Might want to make this a second PR since we want to get this into the release today, if you don't have time.
Other then the tests, this LGTM
@rhatdan there is a good test already for non fully qualified image pull by sha.

:pushpin: Commit 5e08f8d has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon 5e08f8d2e30138c234071ae2e3ed20b590d0fd2e -->

> and like docker, we provide a 'none' tag as well.

Which part of Docker does that? I see
```console
# docker pull alpine@sha256:7043076348bf5040220df6ad703798fd8593a0918d06d3ce30c6c93be117e430
…
Status: Downloaded newer image for docker.io/alpine@sha256:7043076348bf5040220df6ad703798fd8593a0918d06d3ce30c6c93be117e430
[root@f28 ~]# docker images
REPOSITORY          TAG                    IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
docker.io/alpine    <none>                 11cd0b38bc3c        7 days ago          4.41 MB
```

where the `<none>` is a placeholder for “nothing”, not an actual tag value.
